title: Add namespace-scoped RBAC and namespace flag
summary: Adds namespace-scoped RBAC support via rbac.namespaceScoped Helm toggle, --namespace bootstrap flag with cache restriction, Role/RoleBinding templates mirroring ClusterRole rules, conditional deployment args, CI validation scenario, and multi-tenant deployment documentation. Core implementation is correct and well-structured. All automated verification passes (Go tests, go vet, helm lint, helm unittest, helm template). Three gaps remain: role_test.yaml lacks individual rule verification tests (only count-based), VitePress sidebar not updated, and docs missing Prerequisites/CRD Installation sections.

tests_checklist.item: Go unit tests pass (8/8 in bootstrap package)
go vet passes with no warnings
helm lint passes with default values
helm unittest passes (55/55 tests across 9 suites)
helm template default produces ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding, no Role/RoleBinding
helm template namespaceScoped=true produces Role/RoleBinding, no ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding
Role rules identical to ClusterRole rules (diff confirms 0 differences)
Deployment args include --namespace only when namespaceScoped=true
Edge cases covered in tests (empty namespace, single entry, syncPeriod preserved)
Individual rule verification tests in role_test.yaml matching clusterrole_test.yaml structure

code_quality_checklist.item: cacheOptions helper is a pure function, easy to test
Follows existing flag registration pattern in bootstrap
Helm conditional guards follow existing webhook.enabled pattern
role.yaml has sync comment referencing clusterrole.yaml
Test style matches existing tests in same files
Feature ID CC-0043 referenced in all new code

security_checklist.item: RBAC correctly scoped (Role vs ClusterRole mutual exclusion)
Namespace flag validated by controller-runtime (no custom validation needed)
No path traversal or injection vectors

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — toggle + conditional templates + flag + helper
Shared bootstrap package serves both operators without code changes
No scope creep beyond requested changes
Documentation placed in existing docs/reference/ directory

dry_yagni_checklist.item: role.yaml rules duplicated from clusterrole.yaml (necessary duplication due to Kubernetes API constraints, documented with sync comment)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Empty namespace string results in no cache restriction (graceful default)
Bootstrap validates required config before flag parsing

defensive_checklist.item: Namespace flag defaults to empty (safe default = cluster-wide)

issues_found.description: role_test.yaml has only 3 tests (render, no-render, rule count) but task 2.2 explicitly requires individual rule verification tests for all 14 apiGroup rules, matching the clusterrole_test.yaml structure which has 16 tests. The count-based assertion (lengthEqual: 14) would pass even if a rule had incorrect apiGroups, resources, or verbs, as long as the total count stays at 14. This makes the test suite weaker for detecting regression in namespace-scoped rules.
issues_found.fix: Add 14 individual rule verification tests matching clusterrole_test.yaml structure: 'should grant full CRUD on keystones', 'should grant get/update/patch on keystones/status', 'should grant update on keystones/finalizers', etc. Each test should use `set: rbac.namespaceScoped: true` and assert the exact `contains` content for each rule block.
issues_found.evidence: role_test.yaml has 3 tests vs clusterrole_test.yaml has 16 tests (14 individual rules + rule count + namespace-scoped exclusion). Task 2.2 explicitly states: '(3) individual rule verification tests for keystones CRUD, status, finalizers, deployments, core resources, events, batch, mariadb, external-secrets, rbac, policy, autoscaling, networking, leases. Follow clusterrole_test.yaml structure.'

issues_found.description: docs/.vitepress/config.ts sidebar not updated with a link to the multi-tenant deployment documentation. The page at docs/reference/multi-tenant-deployment.md exists but is unreachable from VitePress site navigation. Task 3.2 explicitly requires: 'Update docs/.vitepress/config.ts sidebar'.
issues_found.fix: Add { text: 'Multi-Tenant Deployment', link: '/reference/multi-tenant-deployment' } to the Reference items array in the sidebar configuration.
issues_found.evidence: git diff main...HEAD -- docs/.vitepress/config.ts produces empty output (no changes). The sidebar Reference section at lines 22-32 has no multi-tenant-deployment entry.

issues_found.description: Documentation missing Prerequisites section and CRD Installation note as required by both task 3.2 and review criteria. Task 3.2 specifies: '(2) Prerequisites - CRDs installed by cluster-admin, namespace exists' and '(5) CRD Installation - note that CRDs are always cluster-scoped'. Review criteria state: 'Documentation covers webhook limitation caveat, CRD installation note'. The doc covers webhook caveat and examples but omits the CRD operational prerequisite.
issues_found.fix: Add a '## Prerequisites' section after '## When to use namespace-scoped mode' containing: (1) CRDs must be installed by a cluster-admin before deploying namespace-scoped operators (CRDs are always cluster-scoped Kubernetes resources), (2) Target namespace must exist. Add a '## CRD installation' section noting that CRD installation remains a cluster-admin concern regardless of RBAC mode and is not affected by the namespaceScoped toggle.
issues_found.evidence: grep -i 'prerequisit\|crd.*install\|cluster.admin' docs/reference/multi-tenant-deployment.md returns no matches. Task 3.2 description explicitly lists 5 required sections, of which sections 2 (Prerequisites) and 5 (CRD Installation) are absent.

issues_found.description: rolebinding_test.yaml is missing the roleRef.apiGroup assertion that the sibling clusterrolebinding_test.yaml includes. The clusterrolebinding test verifies roleRef.apiGroup=rbac.authorization.k8s.io but the rolebinding test does not, creating an inconsistency between paired test files.
issues_found.fix: Add `- equal: { path: roleRef.apiGroup, value: rbac.authorization.k8s.io }` to the 'should render RoleBinding referencing Role' test assertions, matching clusterrolebinding_test.yaml line 9-10.
issues_found.evidence: clusterrolebinding_test.yaml:9-10 has `- equal: { path: roleRef.apiGroup, value: rbac.authorization.k8s.io }` but rolebinding_test.yaml has no apiGroup assertion.

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a Helm chart validation (e.g., via helm-unittest or a CI check) that fails when rbac.namespaceScoped=true and webhook.enabled=true simultaneously, since this combination produces an invalid deployment (namespace-scoped RBAC cannot manage cluster-scoped webhook configurations).
next_steps: Add 14 individual rule verification tests to role_test.yaml matching clusterrole_test.yaml structure
Add sidebar link in docs/.vitepress/config.ts for multi-tenant deployment doc
Add Prerequisites and CRD Installation sections to docs/reference/multi-tenant-deployment.md

detected_patterns.name: Helm RBAC conditional guard with sync-required comment
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new Helm chart that supports both cluster-wide and namespace-scoped RBAC deployment modes, or adding new RBAC rules to an existing dual-mode chart
detected_patterns.description: When a Helm chart supports both ClusterRole and namespace-scoped Role, the rules are duplicated across two template files (clusterrole.yaml and role.yaml) with mutually exclusive conditional guards: `{{- if not .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}` for ClusterRole and `{{- if .Values.rbac.namespaceScoped }}` for Role. The Role template includes a sync comment '# Rules MUST be kept in sync with clusterrole.yaml' to flag the coupling. The corresponding bindings (ClusterRoleBinding/RoleBinding) use matching guards. A values.yaml toggle `rbac.namespaceScoped: false` controls the mode. Deployment template conditionally injects `--namespace={{ .Release.Namespace }}` arg when namespaceScoped is true. Tests verify mutual exclusion (document count 0 when mode is off) and rule count parity.
